must_have_breakdown.skill: System Requirements writing and System Architecture Design
RADAR System (End User function) and Product knowledge
Experience on system service function (Diagnostics, Flashability, FOTA, etc) development
Basic Functional Safety and Cyber Security knowledge
Experience in RADAR function validation (HIL and vehicle environment)
Requirement management tools DOORs, Code Beamer and Configuration management tools such as CoreALM

reasoning: Reject — wrong domain entirely. This candidate is a Transport Manager (corporate employee logistics) with 10+ years in facilities and vendor management. The JD demands a Senior System Radar Function Owner with deep RADAR system engineering expertise, ASPICE/ISO26262 compliance knowledge, embedded systems development, automotive communication protocols (CAN/LIN/Ethernet), and HIL validation experience. Not a single must-have skill is present in this resume. There is zero overlap with automotive systems engineering. Do not engage this candidate for this role under any circumstances.
strengths: OPERATIONAL SCALE: Managed end-to-end transportation operations for 2000+ employees across multiple large corporates (Microsoft, Novartis), demonstrating high-volume operational ownership — irrelevant to this JD but shows managerial capability.

CAREER PROGRESSION: Promoted four levels (Executive to Deputy Manager) at Microsoft India over 7 years, indicating performance-driven growth and stakeholder trust — again, entirely outside the automotive systems engineering domain required here.

VENDOR AND BUDGET MANAGEMENT: Demonstrated budget forecasting, accruals management, and procurement collaboration across multiple blue-chip clients — valuable in facilities roles but carries zero transferable value for a RADAR function owner position.
gaps: ZERO AUTOMOTIVE/RADAR DOMAIN EXPERIENCE: The candidate has no exposure to automotive systems engineering, RADAR technology, embedded systems, ASPICE, ISO26262, or any related automotive standard — this is a complete domain mismatch that cannot be bridged through any screening or verification step.

NO TECHNICAL ENGINEERING BACKGROUND: The candidate holds only a Diploma in Computer Science (2012) with no engineering practice evidence; the JD requires system architecture design, requirements writing in DOORs/CodeBeamer, and HIL validation — skills that are entirely absent from this profile.

scoring_factors.reason: No notice period stated and JD specifies no maximum — no penalty applied.
JD requires In-office presence in Chennai; candidate is based in Hyderabad (Beeramguda).
Candidate has 10+ years total experience, well above the minimum threshold of 2 years (half of 4 relevant years required).
JD gender preference is Any — no penalty applied.
No college preference or exclusion specified in the JD — no penalty applied.

availability_reason: Current role at Novartis started May 2025 but resume is active; no notice period stated — availability unclear, defaulting to unknown but listed as current.
self_reported_unverified: Claims management of 2000+ employees transport at both Microsoft and Novartis — scale figures are not independently verifiable from document content.
Multiple promotions at Microsoft (Executive to Deputy Manager) cited as performance-based — no supporting documentation or metrics provided to validate promotion criteria.
interview_flags: This candidate should not be interviewed for this role — confirm whether they applied to the correct job posting before any outreach.
If candidate claims any automotive or engineering background verbally, probe for specific project names, standards worked on, and tools used — resume provides zero supporting evidence.
Clarify the Nov 2024 to Feb 2025 gap between Northern Tools and TJX roles.
Clarify the May 2025 TJX exit and immediate Novartis start — overlapping timelines need explanation.
If repurposing this candidate for a facilities/transport role internally, probe actual budget ownership size and whether the Deputy Manager title involved direct reports.
